title: Workshop of Campin, Annunciation Triptych (Merode Altarpiece)
topic_path: Workshop of Campin, Annunciation Triptych (Merode Altarpiece)

assessment_items.question_content:
**The small size of the altarpiece suggests that . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The small size of the painting suggests that the painting was commissioned by a wealthy individual and that they wanted it for their home.**

choices.content: the painting was made for a private home instead of a public church altar.
the painting was made for patrons who needed to move frequently.
the painting was for the altar of a small parish church.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The painting contains many subtle and overt symbols of Christian belief.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fce6a64a14107d352a0a3b173bcb16df.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The painting contains many subtle and overt symbols of Christian belief.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of Campin's *Merode Altarpiece*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Campin painted the patron (not himself) kneeling in prayer in the panel on the left.**

choices.content: Campin used oil paint and its related techniques, which allowed him to achieve effects that were not possible with tempera.
Campin's work, as a craftsman and painter, is reflected in the tools and activities of Joseph's workshop.
The scene of the Annunciation takes place in a contemporary Flemish home.
Campin painted himself kneeling in prayer in the panel on the left.

hints: **Campin used multiple view points in this painting. Linear perspective had been rediscovered by Brunelleschi and was just beginning to be used in Florence at this time (but not in Northern Europe yet).**
